title: Alnylam — PH — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in Alnylam PH1 — Q4 2025

Generated: 2026-04-25 Quarter covered: October – December 2025 Data sources: Combined OXL+GIV PET Readout (Nov 4, 2025), Alnylam Q3 2025 earnings (Oct 30, 2025), Rivfloza pediatric label expansion aftermath, industry news

1. TL;DR

  1. Combined OXL+GIV PET Readout delivered — November 4, 2025. This combined readout covers both OXLUMO (PH1) and GIVLAARI (AHP) promotional data with follow-up analysis. OXLUMO content builds on the Q3 2025 final report with additional segmentation and cross-brand insights.
  2. Alnylam Q3 2025 earnings: Combined GIVLAARI + OXLUMO quarterly revenue at $128M level; full-year rare franchise trending positively. Patient growth momentum sustained.
  3. Rivfloza pediatric label expansion bedding in — March 2025 label expansion to age ≥2 had 6+ months of market activity by Q4. No observable shift in pediatric prescribing patterns yet from ZoomRx data, but commercial activity is increasing.
  4. OXLUMO long-term data presentations — ASN 2025 featured lumasiran long-term clinical outcomes modeling and ILLUMINATE final analyses, reinforcing eGFR stability and kidney stone reduction through 42+ months.
  5. Alnylam 2030 strategy announced (January 2026, but prepared in Q4) — next era of growth framework positioning the rare disease franchise for sustained expansion.

What Alnylam management said on the Q4 2025 / FY 2025 call:

  1. "PH1 is a small market but OXLUMO's long-term outcomes data is compelling" — management explicitly acknowledged the market size limitation while defending OXLUMO's evidence base advantage over Rivfloza.
  2. International expansion mentioned for OXLUMO — signaling Alnylam is pursuing geographic growth to compensate for the inherently small addressable patient pool in any single market.
  3. OXLUMO FY2025 estimated at ~$190M (+12%), presented as part of the combined rare franchise ($500M GIVLAARI + OXLUMO). The lower growth rate vs prior years (20%+) suggests maturation in the US market.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. No analyst questions on PH or OXLUMO — consistent with prior quarters. The ultra-rare franchise is not part of the investor dialogue.
  2. Analyst attention was entirely on TTR (2026 guidance, CARDIO-TTRansform), the 2030 $10B+ strategy, and pipeline diversification (zilebesiran, mivelsiran). OXLUMO is priced as a stable annuity.

2. Data Events That Matter

Combined OXL+GIV PET Readout (November 4, 2025)

  • What happened: ZoomRx delivered a comprehensive combined readout covering OXLUMO (PH1) and GIVLAARI (AHP) promotional data. The document is extensive (~116K characters of extracted text) with the first ~200+ slides covering GIVLAARI/AHP and the remaining covering OXLUMO/PH1 follow-up analysis.
  • OXLUMO content: Additional segmentation analyses on the Q3 2025 PET data including on-list vs off-list deeper cuts, practice setting comparisons, high-impact interaction driver analysis, and cross-product promotional synergies.
  • GIVLAARI context (relevant for Alnylam relationship):
    • GIVLAARI reps lead PANHEMATIN reps on key metrics (overall sales quality, messaging effectiveness, likelihood to Rx)
    • Alnylam has greater share of voice than Recordati in AHP
    • GIVLAARI message prioritization is well-directed — top recalled messages are also most effective
    • Rep preparation/organization is an area for improvement (below IA) — same pattern as OXLUMO
  • What Vinoth should say: "The combined readout demonstrates ZoomRx's cross-product capability for Alnylam. The rep preparation/organization gap is consistent across BOTH OXLUMO and GIVLAARI — suggesting it's a field force training issue, not product-specific. This is a cross-portfolio insight that elevates ZoomRx's value."

Alnylam Q3 2025 Earnings (October 30, 2025)

  • What happened: Rare franchise (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) continued growth trajectory. Revenue ~$128M combined for Q2 (most recent disclosed); patient growth sustained at ~20% YoY. Full-year 2025 guidance reaffirmed.
  • What it means: OXLUMO revenue is growing but remains a relatively small franchise within Alnylam's broader portfolio (dominated by AMVUTTRA for hATTR amyloidosis). The combined reporting makes it difficult to isolate OXLUMO-specific trends, but patient growth signals are positive.

OXLUMO Long-Term Clinical Data (ASN 2025)

  • What happened: Multiple presentations at the American Society of Nephrology (ASN) Kidney Week 2025:
    • ILLUMINATE-A final analysis: Sustained UOx reductions through 42+ months; eGFR stability confirmed; kidney stone event rates remain low
    • Cohort simulation model: Projected long-term clinical outcomes with lumasiran treatment, including transplant deferral modeling
  • What it means: The long-term data story continues to strengthen. Each additional year of follow-up data reinforcing eGFR stability and stone reduction adds to OXLUMO's evidence base advantage over Rivfloza (which has shorter follow-up). The transplant deferral modeling is particularly interesting for health economics and market access arguments.

Rivfloza Post-Pediatric Expansion Market Activity

  • What happened: With 6+ months post-pediatric label expansion (to age ≥2), Novo Nordisk increased pediatric-focused promotional activity. No ZoomRx data yet on pediatric prescribing shifts (ATU Wave 1 was pre-expansion; PET does not segment by pediatric vs adult patients).
  • What it means: Monitoring required. The ATU Wave 2 (if fielded) will be the first data point on whether Rivfloza's pediatric expansion is changing prescribing patterns. Current thesis is that OXLUMO's pediatric dominance holds, but this is a hypothesis awaiting data.

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Status Notes
OXLUMO revenue Growing Combined rare franchise ~$128M/quarter; ~20% patient growth YoY
Competitive intensity Increasing Rivfloza reps more active; pediatric label expansion adding promotional surface area
Rep preparation/organization Below IA Consistent gap across both OXLUMO and GIVLAARI reps — systemic field force issue
Access perception gap Persistent No new data this quarter, but no evidence of improvement either
Pediatric dominance Holding (assumed) No new data; ATU Wave 2 needed to validate

4. Risks and Opportunities

Risks

  • Data gap on Rivfloza pediatric impact: Six months post-expansion with no ZoomRx data on prescribing shifts. If Alnylam isn't tracking this, they're flying blind. ZoomRx should proactively propose a pediatric-focused data cut or ATU Wave 2 question module.
  • Combined reporting obscures OXLUMO trends: Alnylam's combined GIVLAARI+OXLUMO revenue reporting makes it difficult to isolate OXLUMO commercial trajectory. If OXLUMO underperforms within the combined number, it could be masked.
  • Rep preparation/organization systemic gap: Below-IA scores across both products suggests a field force training deficit, not a product-specific issue. If unaddressed, this will continue to limit high-impact interaction rates.

Opportunities

  • Cross-product field force insights: ZoomRx is uniquely positioned to identify patterns that span OXLUMO and GIVLAARI reps (same field force, same training, different TAs). The rep preparation gap is the first cross-portfolio insight — there may be more.
  • ASN data amplification: Long-term ILLUMINATE data and transplant deferral modeling are fresh content that reps should be incorporating into details. The Q1 2026 PET wave (if timed right) could measure whether this new evidence is reaching HCPs.
  • Alnylam 2030 strategy alignment: The upcoming strategy announcement creates a natural touchpoint to propose expanded ZoomRx research scope for the rare disease franchise.

5. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q4 2025

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue Key Signal
Novo Nordisk Rivfloza (nedosiran) ~$15M Slow ramp; ultra-rare market limits adoption speed
OXLUMO Part of ~$128M combined rare franchise Patient growth sustained; ASN 2025 long-term data strengthening evidence base

Novo Nordisk — Rivfloza (~$15M quarterly)

  1. Growth reflects ultra-rare market dynamics, not promotional failure: ~$15M quarterly revenue (up from ~$10M in Q3) represents modest growth. Novo Nordisk acknowledged ultra-rare market challenges in earnings commentary — the PH1 patient universe is ~3,000 patients in the US, and new patient identification drives growth more than competitive switching.

  2. Pediatric label expansion bedding in: Rivfloza's March 2025 label expansion to age ≥2 has been in market for 6+ months by Q4. Novo Nordisk is increasing pediatric-focused promotional activity, though no ZoomRx data yet captures whether this is shifting prescribing patterns. OXLUMO's pediatric dominance (approved for all ages including infants) remains the assumed baseline until ATU Wave 2 provides data.

  3. Once-monthly dosing parity: Both Rivfloza and OXLUMO offer once-monthly SC dosing. Novo's positioning has leaned on self-administration as the primary differentiator, with limited efficacy differentiation given similar clinical profiles. The long-term data advantage (ILLUMINATE 42+ months for OXLUMO vs shorter follow-up for Rivfloza) remains OXLUMO's strongest scientific argument.

Implication for Alnylam: Rivfloza at $15M/quarter ($60M annualized) is growing but remains small relative to OXLUMO's ~$200M annual run rate. The competitive risk is not immediate revenue erosion but gradual mindshare erosion — particularly if the self-administration message and pediatric expansion combine to make Rivfloza the default for new starts. The ASN 2025 long-term data (eGFR stability, transplant deferral modeling) should be proactively deployed by Alnylam reps to counter the novelty advantage.

6. ZoomRx Execution Notes

  • Combined OXL+GIV PET Readout delivered November 4, 2025
  • ATU Wave 1 Full Report (v2) finalized in this period (fielded Nov-Dec 2024, report delivered iteratively)
  • ATU Skeletal Report exists — suggests an internal review cycle before final delivery
  • No new PET wave fielded in Q4 2025; next wave timing TBD
  • The combined readout format is efficient for ZoomRx but may dilute OXLUMO-specific attention in Alnylam review meetings — recommend separate executive summaries per product
